context: Clark was serving as National Security Advisor; Reagan moved him to Interior via recess appointment after Senate recessed.

position: Assistant Secretary of State for Western Hemisphere Affairs
context: Senate Democrats blocked floor vote; Bush used recess appointment during New Year recess.
Senate blocked confirmation vote over allegations Bolton intimidated intelligence analysts; Bush bypassed Senate during August recess.

position: U.S. Court of Appeals, Fifth Circuit
context: Senate Democrats filibustered nomination; Bush used recess appointment. Pickering later withdrew from future consideration.
Senate Republicans blocked floor vote, objecting to Hormel's advocacy for gay rights; Clinton used Memorial Day recess appointment.

position: U.S. Court of Appeals, Fourth Circuit
context: First African-American judge on the Fourth Circuit; Clinton used recess appointment after Senate failed to schedule hearing.
Senate held pro-forma sessions to block recess; SCOTUS later ruled similar appointments invalid in NLRB v. Noel Canning (2014).
One of three NLRB recess appointments made on same day as Cordray; all invalidated by SCOTUS in NLRB v. Noel Canning (2014).
Part of batch NLRB recess appointments; Senate held pro-forma sessions; SCOTUS invalidated in NLRB v. Noel Canning (2014).

position: Administrator, Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
context: Republicans threatened extended confirmation battle over Berwick's support for rationing concepts; Obama used July 4th recess appointment.
AFL-CIO and Senate Democrats blocked confirmation over Scalia's opposition to ergonomics regulations; Bush used recess appointment.
Not a recess appointment — designated under Vacancies Reform Act after Sessions resignation; included here for context on bypass mechanisms.
Meese nomination was delayed over a year by Senate investigations into financial improprieties; confirmed ultimately via standard vote after recess appointment expired.

position: Assistant Attorney General for Civil Rights
context: Senate Republicans blocked confirmation vote over Lee's support for affirmative action; Clinton appointed him acting assistant AG, then formally recess-appointed.
Senate Republicans blocked cloture votes repeatedly; Obama used recess appointment after Senate left for holiday break.
